concepts.title: Rise of Congress System
concepts.description: The Congress system refers to the period from the late 19th century to the early 20th century when the Indian National Congress played a dominant role in Indian politics. It is characterized by the emergence of the Congress as the main opposition to the British rule and its eventual transformation into a mass movement.

concepts.title: Key Features of One-Party Dominance
concepts.description: One-party dominance is characterized by the existence of a single dominant political party, which has a strong organizational base, a well-defined ideology, and a leadership that is able to command the loyalty of the party members and the masses.

concepts.title: Role of J.L. Nehru in Shaping the Congress System
concepts.description: Jawaharlal Nehru played a crucial role in shaping the Congress system. He was a key leader of the Congress and his leadership style, which emphasized the importance of mass mobilization and democratic participation, helped to transform the Congress into a mass movement.

concepts.title: Congress System Mnemonic
concepts.description: To remember the key features of the Congress system, use the following mnemonic: 'Mass Participation, Democratic Leadership, and National Integration'. This will help you to recall the three main characteristics of the Congress system.

concepts.title: Confusing Congress System with One-Party Dominance
concepts.description: Many students confuse the Congress system with one-party dominance. However, the Congress system refers to a period in Indian history when the Congress was the main opposition to the British rule, while one-party dominance refers to a situation where a single party has a monopoly on power and controls the government and the opposition.

concepts.title: Key Events of the Congress System
concepts.description: Some of the key events of the Congress system include the Lucknow Pact (1916), the Non-Cooperation Movement (1920-1922), and the Quit India Movement (1942). These events helped to shape the Congress system and its relationship with the British government.

concepts.title: Role of Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel in Maintaining One-Party Dominance
concepts.description: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel played a crucial role in maintaining one-party dominance in India after independence. He worked tirelessly to integrate the princely states into the Indian Union and to consolidate the Congress party's power.

concepts.title: Characteristics of One-Party Dominance in India
concepts.description: The one-party dominance in India is characterized by the existence of a single dominant party, the Indian National Congress, which has a strong organizational base and a well-defined ideology. The party has a strong leadership and a massive electoral base, which enables it to control the government and the opposition.
